summary: Operate Azure Key Vault at the vault-container level: tag hygiene, one-way enablement of purge protection, and soft-delete retention tuning. Keys, secrets, certificates, and access policies inside a vault are key material or access control and are engineer-authored, never self-service. Provisioning a brand-new vault also routes to an engineer.

operations.title: Update key vault tags
operations.summary: Set or update this key vault's tags. Only the tags you list change; others are left alone. No effect on secrets, keys, or access.
operations.description: Sets or merges cost-allocation and ownership tags on the key vault. Non-disruptive, takes effect immediately, and has no effect on the vault's stored keys, secrets, certificates, access policies, or network configuration.

params.name: tags
params.label: Tags
params.help: Key/value tags to set; existing keys not listed here are left untouched.

operations.title: Enable key vault purge protection
operations.summary: Turn on purge protection so a soft-deleted vault or secret cannot be permanently purged before retention ends. One-way; cannot be undone.
operations.description: Sets purge_protection_enabled to true on the key vault. With purge protection on, a soft-deleted vault (and its keys/secrets/certificates) cannot be permanently purged by anyone — including an administrator — until the soft-delete retention period expires, protecting against both accidental and malicious permanent deletion. This operation only offers turning it on; Azure does not allow purge protection to be disabled once enabled, so this change is irreversible.

params.name: key_vault
params.label: Key vault
params.help: The key vault to enable purge protection on.

params.name: purge_protection_enabled
params.label: Purge protection enabled
params.help: Whether a soft-deleted vault or secret can be permanently purged before its retention period ends. This operation only offers true (protected); Azure does not permit turning it back off.

decisions: Irreversible: Azure does not allow purge protection to be disabled once enabled. Confirm the vault should be permanently protected before applying — you cannot undo this without deleting and recreating the vault after its retention window.
Purge protection is a durability/anti-deletion control, not an access control; it does not change who can read the vault's secrets.

operations.title: Set key vault soft-delete retention
operations.summary: Set how many days a soft-deleted vault or secret stays recoverable before permanent removal. Longer is safer; shorter narrows the window.
operations.description: Sets soft_delete_retention_days on the key vault — the number of days (7 to 90) that a soft-deleted vault or secret is retained and recoverable before it becomes eligible for permanent purge. A longer window gives more time to recover from an accidental delete; a shorter window reduces how long deleted material lingers. In-place and non-disruptive to the vault's active contents.

params.name: key_vault
params.label: Key vault
params.help: The key vault whose soft-delete retention to set.

params.name: soft_delete_retention_days
params.label: Soft-delete retention (days)
params.help: Days a soft-deleted vault or secret stays recoverable before eligible for permanent purge. Azure allows 7 to 90.

decisions: Lowering the retention window shortens how long a deleted vault or secret can be recovered — confirm no recovery process depends on the current window before reducing it.
If purge protection is enabled, deleted material is retained for the full window regardless; this setting defines that window's length.

operations.title: Provision a new key vault
operations.summary: Request a new key vault with purge protection on, RBAC required, and public network access blocked. An engineer reviews it first.
operations.description: Create a key vault the way this estate builds every one: purge protection on from birth, a deliberate soft-delete retention window, and RBAC controlling who can read its contents instead of vault access policies. Submitting routes the complete specification to an engineer with a draft of the resource to review and finish. Keys, secrets, certificates and access policies inside the vault are never captured here — they are separate, engineer-authored follow-ups once the vault exists.

params.name: key_vault_name
params.label: Key vault name
params.help: Letters, numbers and dashes, 3 to 24 characters, starting with a letter and ending with a letter or number. Key vault names are globally unique across Azure.

params.name: resource_group
params.label: Resource group
params.help: The resource group this key vault belongs to.

params.name: tenant_id
params.label: Azure AD tenant ID
params.help: The engineer fills in the estate's Azure AD tenant ID during review.

params.name: sku_name
params.label: Pricing tier
params.help: Standard covers software-protected keys and secrets; Premium adds hardware-backed (HSM) keys at a higher cost.

params.name: rbac_authorization_enabled
params.label: RBAC authorization
params.help: Always on for a new vault — written into the draft, never asked. Azure role assignments control who can read or manage secrets, not the vault's own access-policy list.

params.name: public_network_access_enabled
params.label: Public network access
params.help: Off by default so the vault has no public endpoint — reach it over a private endpoint. Choosing On exposes it to the public internet; pick On only with a reason a reviewer will read.

params.name: purge_protection_enabled
params.label: Purge protection
params.help: Always on for a new vault — written into the draft, never asked. Azure never allows this to be turned off again once set, so a soft-deleted vault or secret can never be purged early by anyone.

params.name: soft_delete_retention_days
params.label: Soft-delete retention (days)
params.help: Days a soft-deleted vault or secret stays recoverable before it is eligible for permanent purge. Azure allows 7 to 90; longer gives more time to recover from an accidental delete.

params.name: resource_name
params.label: Key vault name tag
params.help: Shown as the name everywhere — consoles, tickets, this portal.

params.name: description_tag
params.label: What this key vault holds
params.help: One line an operator can act on at 3 AM.

params.name: pic
params.label: Person in charge
params.help: Who to call about this key vault.

decisions: Public network access is blocked by default — confirm a private endpoint already reaches this vault, or the engineer relaxes it consciously during review.
Purge protection is on from creation and can never be turned off again — confirm this vault should be permanently protected before applying.
Access policies and RBAC role assignments granting who can read or manage secrets are not captured here — grant them as a separate, engineer-authored follow-up once the vault exists.
Confirm the chosen region matches where the rest of the workload runs.
